tracks.comment:
I'm back from CDMX after recording new episodes of our podcast. Cancioneros is back in April 27 and I went to visit the heart of Latin America to record a bunch of episodes. That's why I wanted to start with one of its best producers: Mexican Institute of Sound (MIS; Spanish: Instituto Mexicano del Sonido — IMS), an electronic music project created by Mexico City-based DJ and producer Camilo Lara. This song is in Politico, his album from 2012. In 2025, Lara was announced as one of the remixers for the 2026 FIFA World Cup theme, representing Mexico City.
Mexican Institute of Sound - Full Performance (Live on KEXP)
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JwHBxC05o0A  
MIS is playin at Pickathon (Oregon) this year Jul 30-Aug2
***
Ya he vuelto de CDMX tras grabar nuevos episodios de nuestro podcast. «Cancioneros» vuelve el 27 de abril y me he desplazado al corazón de Latinoamérica para grabar varios episodios. Por eso quería empezar con uno de sus mejores productores: Mexican Institute of Sound (MIS; en español: Instituto Mexicano del Sonido — IMS), un proyecto de música electrónica creado por el DJ y productor Camilo Lara, afincado en Ciudad de México. Esta canción está en Politico, su álbum de 2012. En 2025, se anunció que Lara sería uno de los remezcladores del tema de la Copa Mundial de la FIFA 2026, en representación de la Ciudad de México.
Mexican Institute of Sound - Actuación completa (en directo en KEXP)
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JwHBxC05o0A  
MIS tocará en el Pickathon (Oregon) este año, del 30 de julio al 2 de agosto

tracks.artist: Mexican Institute of Sound, Meridian Brothers
tracks.comment:
Not only will Camilo Lara return to the Pacific Northwest in 2026 for Pickathon — he’s also back with a new supergroup alongside Colombian band Meridian Brothers: Ruido Tovar.

Ruido Tovar’s album will be released on May 22 via legendary NYC Latin indie label Ansonia Records. During my recent trip to Mexico, I sat down with Meridian Brothers’ Eblis Álvarez, who recommended this track — and also the new Conjunto Media Luna album we’ll hear later. Both Camilo and Eblis will appear on Cancioneros 2026, premiering in April 27.

The Ruido Tovar project draws inspiration from Rigo Tovar and the tropical sounds of the late ’70s and early ’80s. The upcoming album even features Beck on two songs. This collaborative album is rooted in Mexican tropical music from that era. Led by neo-tropical and post-cumbia innovators Eblis Álvarez and Camilo Lara, the project explores the sonic shift that happened when Mexican musicians began moving from traditional instruments to synthesizers.
***
Camilo Lara no solo volverá al noroeste del Pacífico en 2026 para el Pickathon, sino que también regresa con un nuevo supergrupo junto a la banda colombiana Meridian Brothers: Ruido Tovar.

El álbum de Ruido Tovar saldrá a la venta el 22 de mayo a través del legendario sello independiente latino de Nueva York Ansonia Records. Durante mi reciente viaje a México, me reuní con Eblis Álvarez, de Meridian Brothers, quien me recomendó esta canción, así como el nuevo álbum de Conjunto Media Luna que escucharemos más adelante. Tanto Camilo como Eblis aparecerán en Cancioneros 2026, que se estrenará el 27 de abril.

El proyecto Ruido Tovar se inspira en Rigo Tovar y en los sonidos tropicales de finales de los 70 y principios de los 80. El próximo álbum cuenta incluso con la participación de Beck en dos canciones. Este álbum colaborativo tiene sus raíces en la música tropical mexicana de esa época. Liderado por los innovadores del neotropical y la poscumbia Eblis Álvarez y Camilo Lara, el proyecto explora el cambio sonoro que se produjo cuando los músicos mexicanos comenzaron a pasar de los instrumentos tradicionales a los sintetizadores.

Song recommended by Eblis Alvarez from Meridian Brothers who played at Vive Latino. Conjunto Media Luna is from Colombia. This track becomes part of the sonic universe of “Sociedad Internacional de la Kumbia,” Conjunto Media Luna’s new album—a record that consolidates a decade of exploration between accordion, drum machines, and synthesizers, paying tribute to cumbieros around the world who continue expanding the boundaries of cumbia toward infinity across multiple aesthetic fronts: dance, graphic art, fashion, and music.
***
Canción recomendada por Eblis Álvarez, de Meridian Brothers, que actuó en el Vive Latino. Conjunto Media Luna es de Colombia. Este tema forma parte del universo sonoro de «Sociedad Internacional de la Kumbia», el nuevo álbum de Conjunto Media Luna, un disco que consolida una década de exploración entre el acordeón, las cajas de ritmos y los sintetizadores, rindiendo homenaje a los cumbieros de todo el mundo que siguen ampliando los límites de la cumbia hasta el infinito a través de múltiples frentes estéticos: la danza, el arte gráfico, la moda y la música.

tracks.artist: Conjunto Media Luna, Amantes del Futuro
tracks.comment:
We were talking about the new musical project between Meridian Brothers and Mexican Institute of Sound, which proposes a dialogue between Mexican and Colombian cumbia. We connected this to another 2026 album that is also exploring that exchange. Conjunto Media Luna’s “Kumbia Dos Pasitos” was one of the examples Eblis mentioned, but there’s another track that introduces one of the new producers shaping Mexico’s slowed-down cumbia scene: Amantes del Futuro, who appears on “Kumbia Tequendama,” a song that had its global premiere on El Sonido just a month ago.
***
We were talking about the new musical project between Meridian Brothers and Mexican Institute of Sound, which proposes a dialogue between Mexican and Colombian cumbia. We connected this to another 2026 album that is also exploring that exchange. Conjunto Media Luna’s “Kumbia Dos Pasitos” was one of the examples Eblis mentioned, but there’s another track that introduces one of the new producers shaping Mexico’s slowed-down cumbia scene: Amantes del Futuro, who appears on “Kumbia Tequendama,” a song that had its global premiere on El Sonido just a month ago.

For their new release, Los Mirlos, the pioneering group of Amazonian psychedelic cumbia, chose Amantes del Futuro to remix one of their most iconic songs.
***
Los Mirlos — Live on KEXP session/Los Mirlos — Sesión en directo en KEXP: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KtcN5JpzAw8
***
Para su nuevo lanzamiento, Los Mirlos, el grupo pionero de la cumbia psicodélica amazónica, eligió a Amantes del Futuro para remezclar una de sus canciones más emblemáticas.

About a year ago, I saw Los Mirlos in New York City, and that’s when I first learned that the new album they’re about to release features a wide range of guest artists bringing fresh interpretations to the historic catalog of these pioneers from Cochabamba. Just a few weeks ago, we got a glimpse of this new vision with “Eres Mentiroso,” the latest collaboration between Los Mirlos and Bomba Estéreo.
***
Hace aproximadamente un año, vi a Los Mirlos en Nueva York, y fue entonces cuando supe por primera vez que el nuevo álbum que están a punto de lanzar cuenta con una amplia variedad de artistas invitados que aportan nuevas interpretaciones al histórico repertorio de estos pioneros de Cochabamba. Hace solo unas semanas, pudimos vislumbrar esta nueva visión con «Eres Mentiroso», la última colaboración entre Los Mirlos y Bomba Estéreo.

We just heard the collaboration between Los Mirlos and Bomba Estéreo, part of what’s coming in 2026. Both artists share the connection of having recorded Live on KEXP sessions, and today they’re among the most beloved by our listeners. This version of “Fuego” was recorded in 2009 at our former studios.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UHdorv8zMK4 
***
Acabamos de escuchar la colaboración entre Los Mirlos y Bomba Estéreo, parte de lo que nos espera en 2026. Ambos artistas comparten el hecho de haber grabado sesiones en directo en KEXP, y hoy en día se encuentran entre los favoritos de nuestros oyentes. Esta versión de «Fuego» se grabó en 2009 en nuestros antiguos estudios.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UHdorv8zMK4

Several years later, Los Mirlos would record a Live on KEXP in Mexico session at El Desierto Casa Estudio. In the interview they gave me during that visit, they talked about the early stages of what would become their new album — the one they’re now releasing, which includes their collaboration with Bomba Estéreo.
***
Varios años después, Los Mirlos grabarían una sesión de «Live on KEXP in Mexico» en El Desierto Casa Estudio. En la entrevista que me concedieron durante esa visita, hablaron de las primeras etapas de lo que acabaría convirtiéndose en su nuevo álbum —el que están lanzando ahora, y que incluye su colaboración con Bomba Estéreo

While Los Mirlos were pioneers of Amazonian psychedelic cumbia, we can travel further upriver to Iquitos to find another true gem of jungle music: Los Wembler’s de Iquitos.

With “La Danza del Petrolero,” we step into that hypnotic pulse of electric guitars and tropical rhythms that shaped an entire generation in the Peruvian Amazon. That same expansive energy allows us to make an unexpected jump to “Bola Bola en el Tres” by Distrolux, a 1975 piece that continues to explore repetition, trance, and the experimental spirit of that era.
***
Si bien Los Mirlos fueron pioneros de la cumbia psicodélica amazónica, podemos remontarnos río arriba hasta Iquitos para descubrir otra auténtica joya de la música de la selva: Los Wembler’s de Iquitos.

Con «La Danza del Petrolero», nos adentramos en ese pulso hipnótico de guitarras eléctricas y ritmos tropicales que marcó a toda una generación en la Amazonía peruana. Esa misma energía expansiva nos permite dar un salto inesperado a «Bola Bola en el Tres» de Distrolux, una pieza de 1975 que sigue explorando la repetición, el trance y el espíritu experimental de aquella época.

Peru is globally known for its psychedelic sound in styles like Amazonian cumbia or chicha, but the country has also been a pioneer in rock, garage rock, and punk. Los Destellos were a Peruvian musical group that blended cumbia and instrumental rock in their early years. They were formed in 1966 in the Rímac district of Lima, Peru.
***
Perú es conocido mundialmente por su sonido psicodélico en estilos como la cumbia amazónica o la chicha, pero el país también ha sido pionero en el rock, el rock de garaje y el punk. Los Destellos fueron un grupo musical peruano que fusionó la cumbia y el rock instrumental en sus primeros años. Se formaron en 1966 en el distrito de Rímac, en Lima, Perú.

tracks.album: The Los Angeles League of Musicians
tracks.comment:
We continue connecting the dots here on Six Degrees at KEXP. After listening to Los Destellos, one of the pioneering groups of Peruvian rock and psychedelic-leaning tropical sounds, we move toward a newer generation that has drawn deep inspiration from that sonic legacy.

From Los Angeles, La Lom — The Los Angeles League of Musicians channels those echoes of Peruvian psychedelia while blending them with a warm, acoustic-driven guitar sound. Their track “Ángeles Point” captures that balance between vintage tropical psych textures and a contemporary indie sensibility.
***
""We continue connecting the dots here on Six Degrees at KEXP. After listening to Los Destellos, one of the pioneering groups of Peruvian rock and psychedelic-leaning tropical sounds, we move toward a newer generation that has drawn deep inspiration from that sonic legacy.

From Los Angeles, La Lom — The Los Angeles League of Musicians channels those echoes of Peruvian psychedelia while blending them with a warm, acoustic-driven guitar sound. Their track “Ángeles Point” captures that balance between vintage tropical psych textures and a contemporary indie sensibility.

That atmosphere leads us naturally to one of the most extraordinary voices currently based in Los Angeles: Gaby Moreno, originally from Guatemala. Together with La Lom, she released “Alma Florecida” in 2024 — a song that feels both nostalgic and timeless, rooted in bolero traditions while still connected to modern psychedelic currents.
***
Ese ambiente nos lleva de forma natural a una de las voces más extraordinarias que residen actualmente en Los Ángeles: Gaby Moreno, originaria de Guatemala. Junto con La Lom, lanzó «Alma Florecida» en 2024, una canción que transmite nostalgia y atemporalidad, arraigada en las tradiciones del bolero pero conectada al mismo tiempo con las corrientes psicodélicas modernas.

We stay with Gaby Moreno for her beautiful interpretation of the classic “Quizás, Quizás, Quizás,” featured on her 2012 album Postales, further deepening this emotional and stylistic thread.
***
Seguimos con Gaby Moreno y su preciosa interpretación del clásico «Quizás, Quizás, Quizás», incluido en su álbum de 2012 Postales, que profundiza aún más en esta línea emocional y estilística.

We close the set with Adrian Quesada, whose Boleros Psicodélicos project explores lush, cinematic arrangements and features some of the most compelling voices across Latin America. Gaby Moreno was part of it and you can watch her also on Adrian Quesada Live on KEXP
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Izoj76PrIVY 
On “Ojos Secos,” joined by Cuco, we hear yet another reinterpretation of psychedelic romanticism, this time through the Amantes del Futuro remix, offering a fresh and unexpected take that connects back to the evolving modern psych sound we’ve been tracing throughout the hour.
***
Cerramos el programa con Adrián Quesada, cuyo proyecto «Boleros Psicodélicos» explora arreglos exuberantes y cinematográficos y cuenta con algunas de las voces más cautivadoras de toda América Latina. Gaby Moreno formó parte de él y también puedes verla en «Adrián Quesada Live» en KEXP
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Izoj76PrIVY
En «Ojos Secos», con la colaboración de Cuco, escuchamos otra reinterpretación más del romanticismo psicodélico, esta vez a través del remix de Amantes del Futuro, que ofrece una visión fresca e inesperada que conecta con el sonido psicodélico moderno en constante evolución que hemos estado siguiendo a lo largo de la hora.

We just heard “Ojos Secos,” and now we return to Volume I of Boleros Psicodélicos, where Puerto Rican singer iLe proves to be the perfect voice to bring these lush, nostalgic boleros to life.
***
Acabamos de escuchar «Ojos Secos» y ahora volvemos al Volumen I de Boleros Psicodélicos, donde la cantante puertorriqueña iLe demuestra ser la voz perfecta para dar vida a estos boleros exuberantes y nostálgicos.

On Volume II, the sonic map expands as Adrian Quesada invites the outstanding Swiss-Ecuadorian duo Hermanos Gutiérrez, whose instrumental piece “Primos” adds a cinematic desert-psych dimension to the project.
***
En el volumen II, el panorama sonoro se amplía gracias a la colaboración de Adrian Quesada con el destacado dúo suizo-ecuatoriano Hermanos Gutiérrez, cuya pieza instrumental «Primos» aporta al proyecto una dimensión cinematográfica de psicodelia desértica.

In my most recent session with Hermanos Gutiérrez, Alejandro and Estevan shared that one of their most meaningful experiences was recording with Natalia Lafourcade, who invited them to collaborate on her album Cancionera, including this beautiful version of “Luna Creciente.”
***
En mi última sesión con Hermanos Gutiérrez, Alejandro y Estevan me contaron que una de sus experiencias más significativas fue grabar con Natalia Lafourcade, quien los invitó a colaborar en su álbum «Cancionera», donde se incluye esta preciosa versión de «Luna Creciente».

tracks.artist: Mon Laferte, Natalia Lafourcade, Silvana Estrada
tracks.comment:
Speaking of Natalia Lafourcade, she opens the Cancioneros 2025 season — following previous openers Mon Laferte and Silvana Estrada. It’s moving to hear how these artistic friendships evolve, like on Mon’s latest album FEMME FATALE, where she joins Natalia on the intimate song “My One and Only Love.”
***
Hablando de Natalia Lafourcade, ella inaugura la temporada de Cancioneros 2025, tras las anteriores artistas que la han precedido, Mon Laferte y Silvana Estrada. Es conmovedor ver cómo evolucionan estas amistades artísticas, como en el último álbum de Mon, FEMME FATALE, donde colabora con Natalia en la íntima canción «My One and Only Love».

After hearing Natalia Lafourcade, Silvana Estrada, and Mon Laferte together, we stay within that Cancioneros universe and move to another powerful collaboration. Mexican songwriter Silvana Estrada joins Brazilian composer Luiza Brina on “Oração 2,” from the album Prece, a deeply spiritual record shaped by prayer-like harmonies and a sense of ritual intimacy.
***
Tras escuchar juntas a Natalia Lafourcade, Silvana Estrada y Mon Laferte, seguimos en el universo de «Cancioneros» y pasamos a otra colaboración impactante. La cantautora mexicana Silvana Estrada se une a la compositora brasileña Luiza Brina en «Oração 2», del álbum «Prece», un disco profundamente espiritual marcado por armonías que recuerdan a una plegaria y una sensación de intimidad ritual.

"That spiritual thread continues with Brazilian jazz pianist Amaro Freitas, whose album Y’Y explores the ancestral sounds and landscapes of Indigenous communities in Brazil. On “Mar de Cirandeiras,” joined by guitarist Jeff Parker, he creates a fluid, meditative dialogue between jazz improvisation and traditional rhythms.
***
Esa línea espiritual continúa con el pianista de jazz brasileño Amaro Freitas, cuyo álbum *Y’Y* explora los sonidos y paisajes ancestrales de las comunidades indígenas de Brasil. En «Mar de Cirandeiras», acompañado por el guitarrista Jeff Parker, crea un diálogo fluido y meditativo entre la improvisación jazzística y los ritmos tradicionales."

tracks.artist: Amaro Freitas, Criolo, and Dino D'Santiago
tracks.comment:
"We close with something very fresh: at the start of 2026, the trio released the album Criolo, Amaro e Dino. The track “Seka” captures their shared vision — rhythmic, spiritual, and rooted in diasporic connections while pushing contemporary Brazilian music into new territory.
***
Terminamos con una novedad: a principios de 2026, el trío lanzó el álbum *Criolo, Amaro e Dino*. El tema «Seka» refleja su visión compartida —rítmica, espiritual y arraigada en las conexiones de la diáspora— al tiempo que lleva la música brasileña contemporánea hacia nuevos horizontes."

After hearing the fresh collaboration between Criolo, Amaro Freitas, and Dino D’Santiago, we stay within Brazil’s vibrant new scene with Ana Frango Elétrico, who will also be representing the country at Pickathon Festival 2026. This is her latest single, “A Sua Diversão.”
***
Tras escuchar la nueva colaboración entre Criolo, Amaro Freitas y Dino D’Santiago, seguimos en la vibrante escena musical brasileña con Ana Frango Elétrico, quien también representará al país en el Pickathon Festival 2026. Este es su último sencillo, «A Sua Diversão».

We couldn’t talk about Brazil’s new generation without honoring Gilberto Gil, one of the most influential figures in Brazilian music history. In late 2025, he invited contemporary artists like Ana Frango Elétrico to reinterpret songs from his iconic catalog, bridging eras and sounds.
***
No podríamos hablar de la nueva generación brasileña sin rendir homenaje a Gilberto Gil, una de las figuras más influyentes de la historia de la música brasileña. A finales de 2025, invitó a artistas contemporáneos como Ana Frango Elétrico a reinterpretar canciones de su emblemático repertorio, tendiendo un puente entre épocas y sonidos.

Gilberto Gil’s legacy also lives on through modern reinterpretations like this Alok & unfazed remix of “Toda Menina Baiana,” showing how his songwriting continues to inspire electronic and global dance scenes today.
***
El legado de Gilberto Gil también perdura a través de reinterpretaciones modernas como este remix de «Toda Menina Baiana» a cargo de Alok y Unfazed, lo que demuestra cómo su obra sigue inspirando hoy en día a las escenas de la música electrónica y el dance internacional.

Speaking of Bahia, we head to Salvador with BaianaSystem, a band that expand the sound of the Baian guitar. This collaboration with Manu Chao blends sound-system culture, Afro-Brazilian grooves, and political energy into a powerful contemporary anthem.
***
Hablando de Bahía, nos dirigimos a Salvador con BaianaSystem, una banda que amplía los horizontes del sonido de la guitarra bahiana. Esta colaboración con Manu Chao fusiona la cultura del sound system, los ritmos afrobrasileños y la energía política en un potente himno contemporáneo.

"From that same universe, this brand-new 2026 refix brings together Pitty, BaianaSystem, Tropkillaz, and Vandal — a high-intensity mix of rock attitude and Brazilian bass culture.
***
Desde ese mismo universo, este nuevo remix de 2026 reúne a Pitty, BaianaSystem, Tropkillaz y Vandal: una mezcla trepidante de actitud rockera y cultura del bass brasileño."

Zuzuka Poderosa is a Brazilian singer, MC, and songwriter originally from Rio de Janeiro, Brazil. She later moved to New York City, where she became closely connected to the global bass / tropical bass / electronic underground scene, collaborating with producers from the U.S., Latin America, and Europe. Her music blends Brazilian funk carioca, baile funk energy, hip-hop attitude, and electronic club sounds, often with a strong feminist and diasporic identity in her lyrics and performance style.

In the international circuit she’s known for collaborations with artists like Captain Planet, Nickodemus, and producers from the global club movement, and for representing a Brazilian urban sound in a transnational context.
***
Zuzuka Poderosa es una cantante, MC y compositora brasileña originaria de Río de Janeiro, Brasil. Posteriormente se trasladó a la ciudad de Nueva York, donde se vinculó estrechamente a la escena underground del global bass, el tropical bass y la música electrónica, colaborando con productores de Estados Unidos, Latinoamérica y Europa. Su música fusiona el funk carioca brasileño, la energía del baile funk, la actitud del hip-hop y los sonidos electrónicos de club, a menudo con una fuerte identidad feminista y diaspórica en sus letras y su estilo de actuación.

En el circuito internacional es conocida por sus colaboraciones con artistas como Captain Planet, Nickodemus y productores del movimiento global de club, y por representar un sonido urbano brasileño en un contexto transnacional.

"His collaboration with Brazilian indie artist Tagua Tagua offers a warmer, dreamlike take on psychedelic pop and groove-oriented production.
***
Su colaboración con el artista indie brasileño Tagua Tagua ofrece una visión más cálida y onírica del pop psicodélico y de una producción centrada en el ritmo."

From there we connect to Oruã, a band with strong ties to the Pacific Northwest. During their last Live on KEXP session, they recommended the album Slacker, which includes this recent collaboration with Caxtrinho.
***
Desde ahí pasamos a Oruã, un grupo con fuertes vínculos con la costa noroeste del Pacífico. Durante su última sesión en «Live on KEXP», recomendaron el álbum *Slacker*, que incluye esta reciente colaboración con Caxtrinho.
***
Live on KEXP/En Vivo desde KEXP: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gbly0UdcRf4

"Another group shaped in part by Seattle’s sonic influence is Margaritas Podridas, who had the change to release music via Sub Pop and just released new material in 2026. I caught up with them recently in Mexico during their appearance at Vive Latino.
***
Otro grupo que se ha visto influido en parte por el sonido de Seattle es Margaritas Podridas, que tuvo la oportunidad de publicar su música con Sub Pop y acaba de sacar nuevo material en 2026. Hace poco me reuní con ellos en México durante su actuación en el Vive Latino."

Staying in that heavy, alternative spectrum, we hear Vondré, another powerful project from the Latin American grunge-leaning scene. Vondre also played at Vive Latino for a special appearance around International Women's Month
***
Sin salir de ese espectro alternativo y contundente, escuchamos a Vondré, otro proyecto impactante de la escena latinoamericana con influencias grunge. Vondré también actuó en el Vive Latino en una actuación especial con motivo del Mes Internacional de la Mujer.

tracks.album: QUE SE JODAN SI NO PUEDEN ASUMIR UNA BROMA
tracks.comment:
We close this set with Gloory Hole, who just released a new album a few days ago. I met with them just last week — and remember, you’ll be able to enjoy Cancioneros 2026 starting April 27.
***
Cerramos esta sesión con Gloory Hole, que acaba de sacar un nuevo álbum hace unos días. Me reuní con ellos la semana pasada; y recordad que podréis disfrutar de «Cancioneros 2026» a partir del 27 de abril.

One of Gloory Hole's fan bands is Dark Chisme. I remember voting for Dark Chisme to open for Gloory Hole in Mexico. This is Christine's new song.
***
Una de las bandas de fans de Gloory Hole es Dark Chisme. Recuerdo que voté para que Dark Chisme fuera el telonero de Gloory Hole en México. Esta es la nueva canción de Christine.

When talking about darkwave, we simply can’t leave out Prayers, whom I had the chance to see in Mexico City at Vive Latino. Their album *CHOLOGOTH* is a must-listen for anyone looking to understand the new Latin darkwave scene. 
***
Si hablamos de darkwave, no podemos dejar de mencionar a Prayers, a quienes tuve la oportunidad de ver en Ciudad de México durante el Vive Latino. Su álbum CHOLOGOTH es imprescindible para comprender el nuevo darkwave latino.

One of our favorite Mexican bands here on the video team is Malcriada. I finally got to meet up with them to film their “Songbook” episode, and they wanted to include this collaboration with Prayers as one of their most memorable moments.
***
Una de nuestras bandas preferidas de Mexico que tenemos con el video team es Malcriada. Finalmente logre reunirme con ellos para grabar su episodio de cancioneros y quisieron incluir esta colaboracion con Prayers como parte de sus momentos mas importantes.

While talking with Malcriada and Gloory Hole, I often heard them mention Belafonte Sensacional, a groundbreaking band on the Mexican scene and the first to perform a “Live on KEXP” set in Spanish—just like Ela Minus, the artist Sean will be kicking off his show with in a few minutes. I remember that Mabe Fratti recommended this collaboration with them to me.
***
Hablando con Malcriada y con Gloory Hole, muchas veces escuche mencionar a Belafonte Sensacional, una banda parte aguas en la escena mexicana y la primera que tuvo su Live on KEXP en espanol, al igual que Ela Minus, la artista con la que empieza Sean su show en unos minutos. Recuerdo que Mabe Fratti me recomendo esta colaboracion con ellos.

In Episode 3 of Cancioneros 2025, you'll hear Mabe Fratti pick her favorite songs and reveal that she'd love to collaborate with Ela Minus from Colombia.
***
En el episodio 3 de Cancioneros 2025, escucharas a Mabe Fratti eligiendo sus canciones favoritas y confesando quer le gustaria hacer una colaboracion con Ela Minus de Colombia.
Hi pals, welcome to the show! Six Degrees Week!

Six Degrees! - lovely guest spot from friend 'o KEXP Helado Negro

https://heladonegro.bandcamp.com/

Six Degrees! - Helado Negro remixes "Coffee" off Sylvan Esso's terrific 2014 debut

https://sylvanesso.bandcamp.com/music

tracks.song: H.S.K.T. (Hercules and Love Affair Bleeps and Vox Mix)
tracks.comment: Six Degrees! - head, shoulders, knees and toes with some more Sylvan Esso
Six Degrees! - blinding track from Hercules and Love Affair's 2008 classic (co-written, and featuring, Anohni on vocals)
Six Degrees! - you had me at "Hercules and Love Affair meets Yazoo"
Six Degrees! - solo debut from Yazoo's Alison Moyet
Six Degrees! - Alison Moyet's Yazoo bandmate Vince Clarke wrote "Dreaming of Me" during his brief, but influential, tenure with Depeche Mode (on their debut LP Speak & Spell)
Six Degrees! - Erasure's debut LP (formed in 1985 by Vince Clarke and Andy Bell)
Six Degrees! - Erasure's Vince Clarke with a remix of Future Islands' "Doves" (off their 2014 record Singles)
Six Degrees! - featuring Future Islands front man Samuel T. Herring

Six Degrees! - featured on BadBadNotGood's LateNightTales compilation series entry

https://latenighttales.bandcamp.com/album/late-night-tales-badbadnotgood
Six Degrees! - Stereolab remixing the great Scottish indie pop group The Pastels (on a truly stacked and adventurous comp)
Six Degrees! - it's Stereolab with BoC, how 'bout them degrees!
Six Degrees! - another adventurous remix off Warp's 1999 10+3 compilation (I should've just the whole album for this show)
Six Degrees! - going straight to the source with J. Spaceman aka Spiritualized
Six Degrees! - Spiritualized on guitar duty for "Dayzed Inn Daydreams"
Six Degrees! - Ariel Pink + Haunted Graffiti
Six Degrees! - both "Round and Round" and "Helicopter" appear on Pitchfork's Top 100 Tracks of 2010
Six Degrees! - Deerhunter's Bradford Cox aka Atlas Sound with the perfect, timeless "Walkabout"
Six Degrees! - duh, obviously I was going to play some Panda Bear (Noah Lennox)
Six Degrees! - Panda Bear and his bros in Animal Collective with "Fireworks"
Six Degrees! - Animal Collective member Avey Tare remixed by band mate Panda Bear
Six Degrees! - featuring Panda Bear on guest vocals from this 2023 record by the Portuguese duo

tracks.song: Step by Step (Axel Boman's in the Air Version)
tracks.comment: Six Degrees! - the original tune from the French duo features Panda Bear on vocals (are you detecting a pattern?)
Six Degrees! - featured on the soundtrack to that Ron Perlman caveman movie! (or, more factually, the connection is Axel Boman)
Six Degrees! - Talaboman = Axel Boman + John Talabot (killer remix by Japanese artist Powder, too)
Six Degrees! - Powder aka Moko Shibata ~rules~
Six Degrees! - John Talabot + Axel Boman remixed by Powder on that "Loser's Hymn" gem

tracks.song: Shelter (John Talabot Feel It Too Remix)
tracks.comment:

CeeLo Green and the rest of the Goodie Mob crew's 1998 sophomore release 'Still Standing' featured André 3000 with OutKast as well as Witchdoctor. Atlanta production team Organized Noize (Rico Wade, Ray Murray and Sleepy Brown) co-executive produced the album.

Big Boi explained the song's title as being Southern slang for a "a girl that is superfine, bad, sexy, intelligent and jazzy all at the same time."
--
According to producer Mr. DJ, the song's lyrics were inspired by a time when he, André 3000 and Big Boi were "reminiscing about old times." The song embellishes a night at Charles Disco, a real life club that the group used to break into during their high school days. André admitted that in reality, on the night the song revolves around, he got so drunk that he was unable to make it into Charles, and so everything that happened in the song "after that was made up."

tagline: 6 Degrees! Tuesday!

tracks.album: Dancing Time: The Best of Eastern Nigeria’s Afro Rock Exponents 1973-77
tracks.comment:
Welcome to Six Degrees Week on EARLY! 

We're starting our journey with The Funkees, a 1970s Afro Rock and funk band from Nigeria. We'll make our way to Simple Minds' "Don't You Forget About Me."

"Breakthrough" is a standout track by The Funkees, a pioneering Nigerian Afro-rock band that rose to prominence in the post-civil war era of the early 1970s.  The Song reflects the band’s unique blend of Western psychedelic rock (influenced by artists like Jimi Hendrix and Santana) with traditional African rhythms and highlife.

tracks.album: Dancing Time: The Best of Eastern Nigeria’s Afro Rock Exponents 1973-77
tracks.comment: The Funkees' cover of War's "Slipping Into Darkness" is one of the most celebrated examples of 1970s Nigerian Afro-rock. Released in 1973 on the EMI label, it served as the A-side to their iconic single "Breakthrough"

tracks.album: The Best of War and More
tracks.comment:
"Spill the Wine" was the breakthrough 1970 hit for Eric Burdon & War, produced by veteran industry figure Jerry Goldstein. 

In 1969, Goldstein discovered the band—then called Nightshift—performing as a backing group for football player and singer Deacon Jones at the Rag Doll in North Hollywood.

Goldstein, known for hits like "I Want Candy" and "My Boyfriend’s Back," saw the band's multi-racial, genre-blending style as the perfect vehicle for Eric Burdon, who was looking for a new musical direction after leaving The Animals.

The Bow Wow Wow cover "I Want Candy," a Strangeloves' original and produced by Kenny Laguna.

The Strangeloves were a fictional band created in 1964 by the New York-based songwriting and production team of Jerry Goldstein, Bob Feldman, and Richard Gottehrer. The trio invented an elaborate story claiming they were three Australian brothers—Giles, Miles, and Niles Strange—who were raised on a remote sheep farm

"Bad Reputation" by Joan Jett was produced by Kenny Laguna. 

Kenny Laguna is a prolific musician and producer best known as the longtime manager and creative partner of Joan Jett. He played a pivotal role in the production of both her debut solo anthem "Bad Reputation" and the 1982 smash-hit cover of "I Want Candy" by Bow Wow Wow.

After Jett was rejected by 23 major labels, Laguna famously co-produced and self-financed her debut solo album. They famously sold the record out of the trunk of Laguna's car before it was picked up for wider release.

Joan Jett covers Tommy James and the Shondells' "Crimson and Clover."

The cover was a natural fit for Jett because her producer and manager, Kenny Laguna, had deep ties to the original. Laguna was a member of Tommy James and the Shondells and played on the original 1968 recording.

Jett notably kept the original lyrics intact, including the line "I don't hardly know her," rather than changing it to "him." At the time, this was seen as a bold, subversive move for a mainstream rock artist.

tracks.album: The Very Best of Billy Idol: Idolize Yourself
tracks.comment:
Billy Idol covers Tommy James and the Shondells' "Mony Mony."

Billy Idol's cover of "Mony Mony" is one of the rare instances where a live remake of a song became a bigger chart success than both its own studio version and the original 1960s hit.

Released in March 1968, Tommy James and the Shondells' "Mony Mony" was a deliberate attempt to create a "throwback" party anthem.

Billy Idol co-wrote "Eyes Without a Face" with guitarist Steve Stevens. 

"Eyes Without a Face" (1984) was the creative peak of the Billy Idol and Steve Stevens partnership, marking a shift from Idol's punk-rock roots to a more sophisticated, moody New Wave sound.

Stevens was responsible for the song's textural depth. He used a Lexicon Prime Time digital delay to create the ethereal, shimmering guitar sounds in the verses.

Steve Stevens collaborated with the Thompson Twins during a pivotal shift in their sound, contributing his signature rock guitar style to their 1985 album Here's to Future Days.

Stevens played lead guitar on the Nile Rodgers-produced version of "Roll Over". This version was notably punchier and more rock-oriented than the band's previous synth-pop material.

Stevens performed with the band at Live Aid in July 1985 at JFK Stadium. Watch their star-studded performance here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=c_KNArtcc9k

In a classic 1980s pop crossover, Alannah Currie (of the Thompson Twins) and her partner Tom Bailey wrote and produced "I Want That Man" for Debbie Harry’s 1989 solo album, Def, Dumb & Blonde.

While Currie co-wrote the lyrics and concept, Tom Bailey handled the production. He brought in a polished, radio-ready sound that helped revitalize Harry’s solo career.

Susanna Hoffs’ cover of "Dreaming" is a full-circle moment for two of the most iconic women in New Wave and Power Pop. The connection between Susanna Hoffs and Debbie Harry goes back to the mid-80s "rivalry" manufactured by the press between The Bangles and Blondie.

Hoffs has always cited Harry as a primary influence. Over the years, the two have shared stages and mutual admiration. Hoffs has described Harry as the "ultimate cool girl" who paved the way for women like her to lead bands rather than just being "frontwomen."

In one of the most famous examples of his "secret" songwriting, Prince gifted the track "Manic Monday" to The Bangles in 1984 under the pseudonym Christopher.

In early 1986, "Manic Monday" reached #2 on the Billboard Hot 100. It was famously kept out of the #1 spot by Prince’s own song, "Kiss."

"I Feel for You" is a rare "triple threat" of musical genius: written by Prince, performed by Chaka Khan, and featuring a signature harmonica solo by Stevie Wonder.

Producer Arif Mardin felt the song needed a "human" element amidst the heavy synthesizers. He called Stevie Wonder, who recorded the chromatic harmonica solo in one take. The solo is often cited as one of the most recognizable harmonica parts in pop history.

Stevie Wonder’s 1970 cover of "We Can Work It Out" is widely considered one of the greatest Beatles covers ever recorded. It appeared on his album Signed, Sealed & Delivered and transformed the acoustic folk-rock original into a high-energy funk-soul anthem.

Paul McCartney has frequently cited Stevie's version as his favorite cover of the song. He loved how Stevie "found a groove" that the Beatles hadn't even considered.

tracks.song: Sgt. Pepper’s Lonely Hearts Club Band
tracks.album: Sgt. Pepper’s Lonely Hearts Club Band
tracks.comment: Released in 1967, Sgt. Pepper reached new heights of creativity and is often cited as the inspiration for other major pop stars of the era, including Stevie Wonder and Marvin Gaye, to push the boundaries of their own studio work.

Booker T. & the M.G.'s released their cover of "Eleanor Rigby" in 1968 on the album Soul Limbo. It is widely considered one of the most inventive instrumental re-imaginings of a Beatles track, stripping away the iconic string octet of the original and replacing it with Stax Records' signature "Memphis Soul" grit.

In 1970, Booker T. & the M.G.'s recorded an entire album titled McLemore Avenue (named after the location of Stax Studios), which consisted entirely of covers from the Beatles' Abbey Road. The cover art even parodied the famous street-crossing photo.

The Clash’s cover of "Time Is Tight" is a rare, high-energy instrumental that bridges the gap between 1960s Memphis Soul and 1970s London Punk.

The Clash recorded the track during the legendary Marquee Studio sessions in 1978. It was a favorite "warm-up" song for the band, who were massive fans of Booker T. & the M.G.'s and the Stax Records sound.

Sandy Pearlman, an American producer best known for his work with Blue Öyster Cult, was brought in by CBS Records to produce the Clash's second album, Give 'Em Enough Rope (1978), which included the single "English Civil War". 

Pearlman was a massive fan of the band's new drummer, Topper Headon, whom he famously dubbed "The Human Drum Machine" for his precision. As a result, Pearlman ensured the drums were mixed significantly louder than the vocals throughout the album.
Sandy Pearlman was the mastermind behind Blue Öyster Cult, serving as their manager, producer, and primary lyricist from their inception in 1967 until the mid-1990s.

Blue Öyster Cult’s cover of "Born to Be Wild" (originally by Steppenwolf) became a cornerstone of their live identity, famously appearing on their 1975 double-live album, On Your Feet or on Your Legs.

The cover was a calculated choice by Sandy Pearlman. Since he is often credited with being the first rock critic to use the term "heavy metal" in a musical context, having his band cover the song that famously contains the lyric "heavy metal thunder" served as a definitive branding move.

tracks.artist: Grandmaster Flash & The Furious Five ft. Steppenwolf
tracks.comment: Grandmaster Flash & the Furious Five released a hip-hop cover of Steppenwolf’s "Magic Carpet Ride" in 1988. It was featured on their reunion album, On the Strength, which marked the return of the group's original lineup. The track features actual contributions from John Kay & Steppenwolf, blending original rock elements with late-80s hip-hop production.

"It's Nasty (Genius of Love)" is a 1982 hip-hop classic by Grandmaster Flash & the Furious Five that served as one of the earliest and most successful uses of a "loop" in rap history. 

Sampling Tom Tom Club's "Genius of Love," Grandmaster Flash took the original's bright, bubbly synth line and the iconic "James Brown" shout-outs, stripping away the quirky vocals to create a perfect rhythmic bed for the Furious Five to rap over.

tracks.album: The Good The Bad and the Funky
tracks.comment:
Tom Tom Club covered Donna Summer’s disco anthem "Love to Love You Baby" for their 2012 anniversary album, Downtown Rockers. 

The cover was a tribute to the "Queen of Disco" following her passing in May 2012. As a band rooted in the New York Post-Punk and New Wave scenes of the late 70s, Tina Weymouth and Chris Frantz viewed Summer as a foundational influence on the danceable, rhythmic music they created with both Talking Heads and Tom Tom Club.

Keith Forsey did the live kick drum on Donna Summer's 1977 hit "I Feel Love". He, along with Summer's vocals, is the only non-electronic live instrument on the track. Forsey also co-wrote and composed the song "Don't You Forget About Me" performed by Simple Minds.

BOOM BABY, SIX DEGREES! From The Funkees to Simple Minds on EARLY!

tracks.song: (We Don’t Need This) Fascist Groove Thang
tracks.comment:
The song later covered by LCD Soundsystem in 2018.
--

When this song was released in 1981, it banned by the BBC due to concerns by Radio 1's legal department that it libeled Reagan.

Martyn Ware of Heaven 17 said, ""We were obsessed with Reagan coming into power and the specter of Margaret Thatcher coming into power and those were some very genuine concerns. The whole world was going to be blown to smithereens. It seems a little melodramatic now, but it was a genuine thing at the time if you remember." 

 The lyrics of the song reference Prime Minister Margaret Thatcher and President Ronald Reagan and include a wry joke about cruise missiles. 

https://tinyurl.com/ytdbun2x
Produced by Peter Walsh, who also worked on Heaven 17's "(We Don't Need This) Fascist Groove Thang".
Utah Saints covering Simple Minds' "New Gold Dream (81-82-83-84)".
"Something Good" contains a vocal sample from Kate Bush's "Cloudbusting", which had been a top-20 UK hit in 1985.

This Kate Bush hit would be sampled in Utah Saints' "Something Good" seven years later.
--
"'Cause every time it rains
You're here in my head
Like the sun coming out
Ooh, I just know that something good is gonna happen"

Hounds of Love is the fifth studio album by English musician Kate Bush, released on September 16th 1985 by EMI Records. It was a commercial success and marked a return to the public eye for Bush after the relatively low sales of her previous album, 1982's The Dreaming. The album's lead single, "Running Up That Hill (A Deal with God)", became one of Bush's biggest hits, giving Bush her second UK number-one single in June 2022.
Will Smith starred in the 1993 film Six Degrees of Separation alongside Donald Sutherland, who made a guest appearance in the music video for Kate Bush's "Cloudbusting".

This song by The Pretenders was mixed by Bob Clearmountain and written by Chrissie Hynde, who was married to Simple Minds' Jim Kerr from 1984-1990.

Chrissie Hynde shared the inspiration for the song during a performance on the BBC Songwriters Circle program in 1999: "I had in mind this kid who used to stand outside the soundchecks on our first tour... I never spoke to him. I remember that the last time I saw him I just left him standing in the snow, I never had anything to say to him. I kind of wrote this for him, so, in the unlikely event that you're watching this, I did think about you."

P.S. I Love You was named after The Beatles' song of the same name.

The Kevin Shields in question is Kevin Shields of My Bloody Valentine.

tracks.album: Keeping the Faith: A Creation Dance Compilation
tracks.comment:
Happy Tuesday and welcome to The Midday Show with the renowned curators Kevin Cole and Kevin Sur and most excellent producer Meerah!  It’s 6 Degrees Week, where each song is linked to the one before it.  There are lots of ways to make connections—collaborations, samples, covers, and many, many more. Join us as we begin with Kevin Shields and My Bloody Valentine and move to The Afternoon Show, with lots of Kevins in between! Thanks so much for being here with us!
-----
Going from P.S. I Love You's novelty song, "Where on Earth is Kevin Shields" to Kevin Shields himself! 
--
Shoegaze pioneers My Bloody Valentine  formed in Dublin, Ireland, in 1983. Since 1987, the band's lineup has consisted of founding members Kevin Shields (vocals, guitar) and Colm Ó Cíosóig (drums), with Bilinda Butcher (vocals, guitar) and Debbie Googe (bass). 
--
Did you know that This Andy Weatherall remix of My Bloody Valentine's "Soon" was ranked at number 1 in NME's list of "The 50 Best Remixes Ever"?

tagline: It's 6 Degrees of KEVIN!

tracks.song: We Have All the Time in the World
tracks.comment: This song was written by John Barry and Hal David and was a James Bond theme song for "On Her Majesty's Secret Service."  It was originally sung by Louis Armstrong.  This lovely cover by mbv was produced by Kevin Shields.

tracks.song: We Have All the Time in the World
tracks.comment:
John Barry chose Louis Armstrong for this James Bond theme song, because he felt he could "deliver the title line with irony".
--
Louis Armstrong was too ill to play his trumpet at the time of recording, and therefore it was played by another musician. It was reportedly Armstrong's' final recording session before passing away in 1971.
This song was first recorded by Louis Armstrong in 1967.  Joey Ramone covered it for his first solo album, "don't Worry About Me," released posthumously in 2002, less than a year after his death.

Carl Hilding "Doc" Severinsen is an American retired jazz trumpeter who led the NBC Orchestra on The Tonight Show Starring Johnny Carson.
--
This is a medley of tunes paying tribute to Doc's personal music idol Louis Armstrong - and includes:   
When It's Sleepy Time Down South
Struttin' With Some Barbeque
Blueberry Hill
Hello, Dolly
Mack the Knife
Sleepy Time (reprise)

Kevin Tyrone Eubanks  is an American jazz and fusion guitarist and composer. He was the leader of The Tonight Show Band with host Jay Leno from 1995 to its original conclusion in 2009.  The previous musician, Doc Severinsen, also led The Tonight Show band.
"Conscience Be Free" contains samples of "You Gots to Chill" by EPMD and the hook from  "Mice Mobsters" by Kevin Eubanks.
This Gang Starr song directly sampled multiple elements of the Donald Byrd-produced "Wilford's Gone" from The Blackbyrds.

tracks.album: Can You Dig It? The Music and Politics of Black Action Films 1968-75
tracks.comment:
The Blackbyrds were formed in 1973 as part of the legendary jazz trumpeter Dr. Donald Byrd’s brainchild to expose college students to the real world of music.
Learn about this important group here: https://www.blackbyrdsmusic.com/about

This song interpolated the bass from The Blackbyrd's "Wilford's Gone."
--
Here's the official music video for the original "Silent Treatment": https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pKveOqw5ukY

Roots drummer and co-producer Questlove told MTV News about this song, which is a rare upbeat tune on How I Got Over: "As far as 'The Fire' is concerned, we wanted to balance the record out also, with a sign of hope, a ray of hope. 'The Fire' is almost an anthem, if you will. It's like a hang-on-in-there, if you will, for us."
--
This upbeat track features soul singer John Legend, who told MTV News he's been a fan of the Philly collective since his college days, when he was a student at the University of Pennsylvania. "This is a group I've been following since I was a kid at UPenn. I would go to open mics and watch them host the open mics, wishing I could be onstage with them, and now I'm in the studio with them," he gushed.

Hear anything familiar?  The drums in this Paul McCartney tune were sampled in The Roots' "The Fire." 
--
"An instrumental recorded completely at home. Made up as I went along – first a sequence of chords, then a melody on top. Piano, drums, acoustic guitar, electric guitar. Originally it was two pieces but they ran into each other by accident and became one." --Paul McCartney

tracks.song: Turn! Turn! Turn! (To Everything There Is a Season)
tracks.comment:
Mary Hopkin, credited on some recordings as Mary Visconti from her marriage to Tony Visconti, is a Welsh singer. Here's a biography: https://www.maryhopkin.com/biography.html
--
Paul McCartney produced this wonderful version of the 1959 Pete Seeger original, made famous by The Byrds in 1965.

"Tribal Gathering" is a space-rock piece composed by David Crosby & Chris Hillman and recorded August 16, 1967 for inclusion on the Byrds' 1968 album, "The Notorious Byrd Brothers." 

This song alludes heavily to the Human Be-in, a festival celebrating this psychedelic culture held in Golden Gate Park on January 17, 1967, a year before this song’s release.

Ron Asheton of The Stooges heavily sampled the guitar riff from The Byrds' "Tribal Gathering." 
---
John Cale produced this song. We absolutely loved it when John Cale graced our studio for a live performance in 2012: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1tEgJk5VfSc

"I got the...RADIO ON!"

Jonathan Richman wrote this two-chord garage anthem in his father's car in 1972. During the summer of 1977 when Punk and New Wave music were finding a wider audience in Britain, this song became a hit.
---
 Richman was obsessed with the Velvet Underground. When he started his own band, he rewrote the Velvets' "Sister Ray" into this song. AND, the producer for "Roadrunner"  was Velvet Underground member John Cale.

Among the Modern Lovers playing on this song were keyboard player Jerry Harrison who later joined Talking Heads and drummer David Robinson, who later joined The Cars.

That's the very talented John Cale playing celeste, piano, and Hammond organ on this gorgeous Nick Drake track. 
---
"I never felt magic crazy as this
I never saw moons, knew the meaning of the sea
I never held emotion in the palm of my hand
Or felt sweet breezes in the top of a tree…"

"Northern Sky" was written when Drake was living with John Martyn and his wife Beverley in Hastings. Beverley later said, "He wrote that one with us. We had a tree across the pavement. Hence the line, 'Smelt the sweet breezes at the top of a tree.'"

tracks.album: Things Behind The Sun (songs of Nick Drake)
tracks.comment:
Nick Drake was long cited as one of the more obvious influences on Beck, especially on "Sea Change. "Then in December of 2005, Beck put up three different Nick Drake covers from his album Pink Moon on his website.
--
One profile explained that Beck was "keen to understand how some of his favorite Drake songs were made," so he went to work recreating them, learning how they work. Since he didn't know what to do with the final results, he put them on beck.com.

This song sampled the Beastie Boys' "So Whacha Want."
--
Did you know that Beck wrote "E-Pro" in segments of 20 seconds—the intro lasts 20 seconds, verse one lasts 20 seconds, the chorus lasts 20 seconds, and so on.
This remix features a sample of "Fido" by The Byrds.

This is Fatboy Slim's cover of "The Joker" by The Steve Miller Band.  It features  bassist and singer Bootsy Collins on vocals. 
--
In this video, Steve Miller talks with Dan Rather about the meaning of "The Joker" and getting writing advice from Paul McCartney: https://www.facebook.com/AXSTV/videos/steve-miller-talks-the-meaning-of-the-joker-lyrics/4679831762139469/

George Clinton wrote "Mothership Connection" with bass player Bootsy Collins and keyboard player Bernie Worrell. 
--
The song came out of George Clinton and Bootsy Collins seeing a UFO one night. "We were in the Bermuda triangle, Collins recalled to Uncut magazine. "We were fishing and getting high and the mothership concept came."
--
This has been sampled many times, mostly by West Coast rappers in the '90s. The most famous song to borrow it is "Let Me Ride" from Dr. Dre's highly influential 1992 album The Chronic.

This song directly samples  multiple elements of Parliament's "Mothership Connection (Star Child)." It also samples Parliament's 1977 version of "Swing Down, Sweet Chariot." and the drums from James Brown's "Funky Drummer."
--
The end of the music video for "Let Me Ride" contains archive footage of P-Funk performing "Mothership Connection" live, including the spacecraft landing.: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cKu3_3mp1U8

tracks.song: Buffalo Stance (Kevin Sanderson’s techno stance remix one)
tracks.comment:
Kevin Saunderson is one of the pioneers of Dtroit Techno. Learn some more about him from the Detroit Historical Society: https://www.detroithistorical.org/learn/online-research/encyclopedia-of-detroit/saunderson-kevin
--
His remix of this classic Neneh Cherry song sampled the drums from James Brown's 1970 "Funky Drummer (Pt. 1 & 2)."

Kevin Saunderson both produced and co-wrote this song, which featured co-writer and vocalist Paris Grey. 
--
He said, "'Good Life' is a song that's going to touch people forever, it's gonna inspire people, change their mood when they need it, it's definitely going to make them dance—whether the original or a remix. It's that kind of song. It wasn't the intent to make a record to be a hit, it was the intent to make a record that could be played in the clubs...."

"You Belong" sampled the hook from Inner City's "Good Life." 
That's Nomi Ruiz on vocals and Anohni on background vocals .

tracks.song: Ready for the Floor (Hot Chip V.I.P. mix)
tracks.comment:
Hot Chip!! This remix of 
Ready for the Floor" was done by Daniel De Mussenden Carey, a London producer/remixer. Carey owns Mr Dan's recording studio and record label Speedy Wunderground .
--
Here's an in-depth interview: https://analoguefoundation.com/voice/dan_carey/

This 2022 song from Kae Tempest was produced by Dan Carey and features Kevin (!!) Abstract from LA hip-hop collective Brockhampton.
--

Live from the Yspirock Music Festival in Italy in 2024!
--
Oh, my!  Watch Kae Tempest perform “People’s Faces” accompanied by Dan Carey, at the Royal Albert Hall: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YVhm_wI-PWY

This song from Tame Impala's debut album was mixed by renowned producer and mixer Dan Carey.
--
Kevin Parker did all the vocals and instrumentation on this track.

tracks.song: From Little Things Big Things Grow
tracks.comment:
The connection is Australian and KEVIN!! ---Kev Carmody is one of Australia’s pre-eminent wordsmiths whose politically charged, and socially aware lyrics have cemented him as one of the country’s contemporary voices. Of Aboriginal and Irish heritage, both cultures famous for oral histories in song, Kev was born to be a story-teller.  Learn more about him here: https://www.kevcarmody.com.au/
--
See singer-songwriters Paul Kelly and Kev Carmody sing a version of their classic Indigenous rights song "From Little Things, Big Things Grow" at the memorial service for Prime Minister Gough Whitlam: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dAONlfoNVuY

An award-winning musician and producer of Wik and Solomon Islander heritage, Ziggy  Ramo's artistry challenges the settler colonialism and institutional racism upon which Australia was built.: https://www.ziggyramo.com/about
--
 ‘From Little Things Big Things Grow’ was originally written by Paul Kelly and Kev Carmody and based on the ‘tale of Vincent Lingiari’ and the Gurindji Strike.
The original was an aspirational folk tale, how a small workers strike grew into the Aboriginal Land Rights Act, immortalized by Paul Kelly’s iconic refrain. 
--
Watch the dramatic performance of this song by Ramo and Kelly: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9Jd3JZb7WVA

tracks.album: Doo Wop (That Thing) [triple j Like a Version]
tracks.comment:
Ziggy Ramo covered Lauryn Hill's classic here. He collaborated with Australia's "harmonizing matriarch," Christine Anu. 
--
"Gender's a construct, but we hold it up (Yeah, yeah)
Are you over it? Have you had enough? (Yeah, yeah)..."
While staying largely true to the original, Ziggy Ramo worked in a few bars for a powerful pre-chorus about the construct of gender and the toxicity that brings

Though the Fugees had been wildly successful, and Lauryn Hill had been widely recognized as a key to their popularity, few were prepared for her stunning debut.  Hill, of Haitian heritage, rhymed in a tough Caribbean patois on the opener, "Lost Ones," wasting little time to excoriate her former bandmates and/or record-label executives for caving in to commercial success. --Allmusic https://tinyurl.com/yc5zeudh

Don't be one of the Lost Ones. Donate to the KEXP Spring Drive!
https://www.kexp.org/donate/

tagline: 6 DEGREES WEEK! Starting with Ms Lauryn Hill!

tracks.comment:
Ms. Hill blazing through the powerful MTV Unplugged No. 2.0 cut, electrifying the originally stripped down song. Stereogum wrote that “it’s a masterful performance, proving that although Hill is an elusive figure, when she gets down to it she’s one of the best to ever do it.”

Watch her perform the song live at Austin City Limits: https://youtu.be/nqEgHnbBSU4
---
https://mslaurynhill.com/

tracks.artist: Wyclef Jean feat. Refugee Camp Allstars
tracks.comment:
From Wyclef Jean’s critically acclaimed debut album, and featuring vocals from Ms. Lauryn Hill. The their review of the album, the AV Club wrote "In his universalist embrace of music of all forms, Wyclef Jean makes a more powerful call for peace and unity than a thousand East Coast–West Coast 'Stop the violence, y'all' intros put together."

Watch Wyclef Jean's official music video for “We Trying To Stay Alive:” https://youtu.be/KPV1InMNUf4

This track samples "Din Daa Daa" by George Kranz and "Voices Inside My Head" by The Police.

"Nappy Heads" was the second single from Fugees 1994 debut studio album 'Blunted On Reality'  John Forte (a member of the Refugee Camp who appears on The Score) talked about how the Fugees were about to get dropped from their label (Ruffhouse/Columbia) before producer Salaam Remi produced a remix for their first album's single "Nappy Heads" that caught fire on radio - leading to the Fugees getting a second album, 'The Score'.

"Nappy Heads" video!
https://youtu.be/s8sNnNRhQ2g

tracks.album: One Day It’ll All Make Sense
tracks.comment: Retrospect for Life is renowned for its introspective lyrics on abortion and parenthood, noted for being a quintessential, uplifting guest appearance for Lauryn Hill.

tracks.song: Never Dreamed You’d Leave in Summer
tracks.comment: Lauryn Hill interpolated and famously covered Stevie Wonder’s "Never Dreamed You'd Leave in Summer" on "Retrospect for Life". Lauryn Hill's soulful vocals embody the hook of this song, which was used to heighten the emotional impact of the track’s subject matter regarding abortion and birth.
So What the Fuss features Q-Tip, who is often cited as a pioneer of the jazz-rap genre alongside Native Tongues, influencing artists like Lauryn Hill, who bridged neo-soul and hip-hop.

Mobb Deep and Lauryn Hill are both architects of 1990s East Coast hip-hop, with Hill's work occasionally echoing the song's gritty themes and lyrical prowess. The raw, unfiltered street storytelling of "Shook Ones" influenced the overall landscape of New York hip-hop that Lauryn Hill and the Fugees navigated. Some discussions point to similar attitudes regarding "fake" street personas.

Get Away by Mobb Deep features a prominent female vocal sample and is often regarded for its emotional depth and vulnerability—a departure from their typically raw street anthems, highlighting a desire to escape stress and chaos.

The Veldt interpolate Mobb Deep's classic "Get Away" on this track
---

The Guardian calls this Raleigh, North Carolina (and NYC) band "pioneer black shoegazers who anticipated The Weeknd": https://www.theguardian.com/music/2016/feb/05/the-veldt-chavis-brothers-apollo-heights-shoegazer-soul-music

--
https://theveldtmusic.bandcamp.com/album/electric-revolution-rhythm-and-drone-ep

The Veldt were in our studio December 2023 (hosted by Larry Mizell, Jr.) for a great performance that began with "The Everlasting Gobstopper", check it out here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AEDwVb_Qcp0
--
"Daniel and his identical twin brother, Danny, hail from Raleigh, North Carolina. After discovering a love of gospel, Motown, and Pink Floyd, the two began playing together in bands as teens. They woodshedded their songs and cut their chops coming up in the burgeoning Raleigh scene with bands like Archers of Loaf, Superchunk, and Squirrel Nut Zippers." https://medium.com/etc-magazine/shoegazing-in-sandals-1820f20e6c9e
---
https://theveldtmusic.bandcamp.com/album/illuminated-1989

The Veldt supported the Cocteau Twins on tour in the U.S. during the early 1990s during the Four Calendar Cafe era. The tour featured The Veldt providing a shoegaze-soul sound, with members of both bands sharing a mutual respect

Elizabeth Fraser (Cocteau Twins, Massive Attack) released "Moses" in celebration of Bristol-based English musician and sound recordist, Jake Drake-Brockman. Drake-Brockman is a close friend of hers and of her partner Damon Reece. Brockman was known to fans as "the fifth Bunnyman", associated with the Liverpool group Echo & the Bunnymen https://elizabethfraser.bandcamp.com/track/moses

This song features the vocals of Cocteau Twins' Elizabeth Fraser, who wrote this song's lyrics inspired by the death of her one-time close friend, singer-songwriter Jeff Buckley.

Massive Attack doesn’t release new music often, but that could change in 2026. On social media, the band shared, “from next year we will release a cache of work created in the recent past,” 
https://tinyurl.com/yw32pa4c

"Spying Glass" is a re-imagining of a track from their 1994 album Protection, featuring reggae legend Horace Andy. It was famously reworked as a "reggae soundclash" by Mad Professor and often serves as a highlight of their live sets, frequently played alongside "Angel" to showcase Andy's distinct vocals.

tracks.album: Ain’t No Sunshine (The Best of Horace Andy)
tracks.comment: Reggae legend Horace Andy recorded a renowned reggae cover of Bill Withers' "Ain't No Sunshine" in 1973. Produced by Bunny "Striker" Lee, this version is celebrated for blending Andy's unique, trembling vocal style with a distinct Jamaican roots reggae feel, setting it apart from numerous other covers of the classic soul song.

Bill Withers recorded on the Sussex label. founded by music executive and businessman Clarence Avant, that existed from 1969 through 1975. Although Bill Withers was the label's best-selling artist, it was also the home base of guitarist Dennis Coffey and others.  Go here for a Sussex Records Discography: https://www.bsnpubs.com/buddah/sussex.html

Grace Jones worked with Sly Dunbar and Robbie Shakespeare on this 1981 reinterpretation of a Bill Withers hit. She initially didn't know it was a Bill Withers song when she recorded it! It was produced by Chris Blackwell.
This iconic album was recorded at Chris Blackwell's studio, on his label Island Records!
A beautiful cover of Bob Marley's "Natural Mystic."

Recorded at studio one!
--
Horace Andy was born Horace Hinds on 19th February 1951, in Kingston, Jamaica. At 16 years old, he recorded his first single "This Is A Black Man's Country" for Phil Pratt. His real recognition came with "Skylarking", which became the number one hit of the time. Little by little, he imposed his very particular style, sweet and sad, engaged and nonchalant. In the early 90s, the group Massive Attack brought him to the forefront of the world stage. 
Learn more about his important artist: https://store.on-usound.com/artist/95848-horace-andy?lang=en_US

Another hit recorded at studio one!
--
"You Don't Love Me (No, No, No)" is a reimagined dancehall version of her own 1967 rocksteady original, which itself was inspired by Willie Cobbs' 1960 blues song. The 1994 comeback single became a global anthem, hitting #3 in the UK and featuring a melody inspired by the gospel hymn "Jesus Loves Me"
Also recorded at studio one!

tracks.artist: Bob Marley & The Wailers, Lauryn Hill
tracks.comment: Although originally released on the 1977 Exodus album, "Turn Your Lights Down Low" was never released as a single during Bob Marley's lifetime. It later became a major hit in 1999 when re-released as a duet with Lauryn Hill (who was then dating Rohan Marley), peaking at number one on the UK R&B Singles Chart.
Cover of the Bob Marley track by the My Morning Jacket frontman. It was released by Starbucks under the Hear Music label.

tracks.album: Little Darla Has a Treat for You, Volume 17
tracks.comment:
Jim James is the founder, lead vocalist, primary songwriter, and guitarist for the American rock band My Morning Jacket.
--
My Morning Jacket covers Erykah Badu's 1997 song "Tyrone"!!

1997 video of Badu performing "Tyrone" live:
https://youtu.be/YY2-mrsXgMM

That's Stephen Marley singing with Erykah Badu on this lovely track. 
--
Over the past 45 years, Stephen Marley has won no fewer than eight Grammy Awards—three with The Melody Makers, three times as a solo artist, and twice as a producer of his younger brother Damian Marley. Every one of his solo projects to date has topped the Billboard Reggae charts.: https://www.stephenmarleymusic.com/my-roots

In late 2023, YG Marley began performing this original song, "Praise Jah in the Moonlight", during his mother Lauryn  Hill's concert tour commemorating the 25th anniversary of "The Miseducation of Lauryn Hill."  Video clips from his performances went viral on social media, most notably on TikTok.  On December 27, 2023, Marley officially released "Praise Jah in the Moonlight", his first single.

→ in the summer of 1996 Hill had met Rohan Marley, and subsequently began a relationship with him, and on August 3, 1997, Marley and Hill's first child, Zion David, was born, Zion David Marley, the first of five she was to have with Rohan Marley including YG Marley.

tracks.artist: Fugees feat. A Tribe Called Quest, Busta Rhymes & Forte
tracks.comment:
"Rumble in the Jungle" (1996) was created for the "When We Were Kings" documentary and features the very first legal sample granted by ABBA, using the bassline from "The Name of the Game". The music video was filmed in one night in a custom boxing ring built in a Brooklyn alleyway:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Ok6fmRt6MvU
Rest In Peace to John Forté, an associate of the Fugees who has passed away at age 50. https://pitchfork.com/news/john-forte-rapper-and-fugees-producer-dies-at-50/

This song is about the police and how they tend to treat Black Americans.

Rest in Peace, Tyre Nichols, avid skateboarder and photographer. https://thiscaliforniakid2.wixsite.com/tnicholsphotography

tracks.song: All My Time (Matty’s/Mousse T’s club mix)
tracks.comment:
Deep cut Lauryn Hill track sampling the bassline from Kool & The Gang's "Too Hot"

https://www.discogs.com/release/22936877-Paid-Live-Feat-Lauryn-Hill-All-My-Time?srsltid=AfmBOoq_bRgcQLEwXOEbSZPZpmOpIgF6Amn-GAEA1D5cULuaqzfGuKme

tracks.comment:
That lovely voice is Lauryn Hill.
--
"If I Ruled the World (Imagine That)" contains samples of the vocals from "Walk Right Up to the Sun" by The Delfonics and "If I Ruled the World" by Kurtis Blow and the hook from "Friends" by Whodini.
--
 See the official video for "If I Ruled the World (Imagine That): https://youtu.be/mlp-IIG9ApU

tracks.artist: SBTRKT, Little Dragon, Drumma Boy, Shabazz Palaces
tracks.comment: Gorilla vs. Bear points to this soft Drumma Boy remix of UK producer SBTRKT's 'Wildfire" (featuring Little Dragon) from SBTRKT's debut LP. It features a pair of appropriately silken verses from recent BNM honoree Shabazz Palaces.

It's 6 Degrees of Yukimi on Drive Time today!
___

Yukimi Eleanora Nagano, is a Swedish singer and songwriter. She is the vocalist for the Swedish electronic group Little Dragon and recently released her first solo album "For You" back in March.

https://yukimi-music.com/

tagline: KEXP Radio hand selects all of its music, with expert DJs showcasing both established and emerging bands. Rock is generally a broad term for KEXP, as indie and alternative sounds are often blended with a variety of other genres in an innovative music format. KEXP variety mix programming makes up the bulk of our broadcast service, bringing listeners an edgy mix of alternative rock, hip hop, electronic, roots & blues, world, jazz and more.

Rostam was born in Washington, DC to Iranian parents.  His name comes from a legendary hero in Persian mythology, the mightiest of Iranian paladins whose acts were immortalized by the 10th-century poet Ferdowski in the epic poem "Shahnameh" ('The Book of Kings')

A drum loop from Paul Simon's 'The Obvious Child' was sampled in the previous Rostam song.

The drums for this song were recorded live at Pelourinho Square in the Brazilian city of Salvador. They were played by the Afro-Brazilian group Grupo Cultural Olodum, who are masters of the heavily percussive sub-style of samba called Batuque.

This was the first rap song to sell over 500,000 copies, earning a certified Gold record. The album was also the first rap record released on a major label. Kurtis signed with Mercury Records early in 1980.

Kurtis told Songfacts: "The concept was created as a tribute to all the breakers in and around the South Bronx and Harlem back in the early days of hip-hop. I wanted to do a tribute song with many breaks so that the breakers could get down and do their thing. When we danced during the breaks of a song, that was our time to go off - to do our best moves."

https://bit.ly/3jX989f

The "Hey Ladies" line comes from Kurtis Blow on his 1983 cut "Party Time."

"Afilando Los Cuchillos" (translates to "Sharpening the Knives") is a protest song by Puerto Rican musicians Bad Bunny, Residente, and iLe. It was released on July 17, 2019 through YouTube, in response to Ricardo Rosselló and the Telegramgate -- the leak of hundreds of pages of a group chat on the messaging application Telegram between Rosselló and member of his cabinet. The messages were considered vulgar, racist, and homophobic toward several individuals and groups, and discussed how they would use the media to target potential political opponents.

This song unites artists from across the world: K'NAAN (Somalia/Canada), Snow Tha Product (USA/Mexico), Riz Ahmed (UK/Pakistan), and Residente (Puerto Rico). Released in 2016, it blends hip-hop and global influences to highlight immigrant struggles and resilience, appearing on The Hamilton Mixtape.

____

Esta canción une a artistas de todo el mundo: K'NAAN (Somalia/Canadá), Snow Tha Product (EE. UU./México), Riz Ahmed (Reino Unido/Pakistán) y Residente (Puerto Rico). Lanzada en 2016, combina hip-hop e influencias globales para resaltar las luchas y la resiliencia de los inmigrantes, apareciendo en The Hamilton Mixtape.

____

Additionally: Residente and Lin-Manuel Miranda are third cousins. The two rappers discovered they were related backstage at a concert in Puerto Rico: “Residente asked Mr. Miranda to introduce Calle 13 at a concert in Puerto Rico, and when Mr. Miranda met Residente’s family there, Residente’s mother instantly recognized the face of his grandfather in his.”

Born Keinan Abdi Warsame, K'naan was born in Mogadishu, Somalia.

tracks.song: (I’ll Love You) Till the End of the World
tracks.artist: Nick Cave and the Bad Seeds
tracks.album: Until the End of the World
tracks.comment: Nick Cave said: “Though the love song comes in many guises – songs of exultation and praise, songs of rage and of despair, erotic songs, songs of abandonment and loss – they all address God, for it is the haunted premises of longing that the true love song inhabits. It is a howl in the void, for Love and for comfort and it lives on the lips of the child crying for his mother. It is the song of the lover in need of her loved one, the raving of the lunatic supplicant petitioning his God. It is the cry of one chained to the earth, to the ordinary and to the mundane, craving flight; a flight into inspiration and imagination and divinity. The love song is the sound of our endeavors to become God-like, to rise up and above the earthbound and the mediocre.”

Fun Busta Rhymes Facts

1. He once held a record in the Guinness Book of World Records.

Busta raps at excessive speeds. I mean really, how many times did you have to listen to his verse on “Look At Me Now” to catch the first three lines of his verse? In 2000, Busta Rhymes was entered into the Guinness Book of World Records for having said the most syllables in one second. 

2. His rap name comes from a former football player.

His first rap name was Chilly-O-Ski. Soon after giving himself that name, he and plenty of other people decided it was corny. Chuck D of Public Enemy named him Busta Rhymes after the former Minnesota Viking, George “Buster” Rhymes.

tracks.album: I'm Still in Love With You
tracks.comment:
Busta Rhymes sampled Al Green's essential track, Love and Happiness in "Turn it Up" 

Of the 1972 recording process for "Love and Happiness", Green said: "I wouldn’t say it was a return to the old style soul of my early days, but I definitely pulled it from the same source of raw and gritty need. The result was like a slow fever, building on the beat, pushing up the temperature with each breath of the staccato horns and pushing through to delirium as we came up on the fade."

Teenie Hodges co-wrote Love and Happiness with Al Green. Mr. Hodges — whose brothers gave him his nickname on account of his height — quickly became an in-demand guitarist in Memphis, playing on Sam and Dave’s “I Take What I Want,” released by Stax in 1965. (Mr. Hodges also received a co-writing credit on that song, along with Stax’s house writers Isaac Hayes and David Porter.) https://www.nytimes.com/2014/06/26/arts/music/teenie-hodges-soul-guitarist-and-songwriter-dies-at-68.html

Hayes and Porter were inducted into the Songwriters Hall of Fame in 2005 in recognition of writing scores of songs for themselves, the duo Sam & Dave, Carla Thomas, and others. In 2002, Hayes was inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame. 

“Theme from ‘Shaft’” changed Hayes’ standing in the recording industry. At the age of 29, he was jettisoned into super stardom and the record books. No longer considered to be just an “album artist,” he was now recategorized as a more valuable commodity: a “singles artist". https://www.loc.gov/static/programs/national-recording-preservation-board/documents/ThemeFromShaft.pdf

Samples the Theme From Shaft! 

Several months before his "Bust A Move," made him a star, Young M.C. released this single. There are multiple samples in this song, including Isaac Hayes' theme from "Shaft" (Yes, you heard it!) and the Incredible Bongo Band's "Apache".: https://www.whosampled.com/Young-MC/Know-How/samples/

tracks.song: Damn I Wish I Was Your Lover
tracks.album: Damn I Wish I Was Your Lover
tracks.comment:
Damn! Richard E. Chertoff (born March 29, 1950) is an American record producer and songwriter. He is credited on the singles Joan Osborne's "One of Us", Cyndi Lauper's "Girls Just Want to Have Fun" and "Time After Time" and Sophie B. Hawkins' "Damn I Wish I Was Your Lover".

One of Chertoff's biggest successes in his Columbia days was producing Lauper's debut album, She's So Unusual. The album sold 9 million copies with hits such as "Girls Just Wanna Have Fun", "All Through the Night", "When You Were Mine" and "Money Changes Everything." He brought together Hyman and Lauper who co-wrote the #1 hit, "Time After Time." Lauper and Chertoff then teamed up to write the hit, "She Bop."

In 1980, Chertoff joined Columbia Records, becoming Senior Vice President of A&R in 1989. He signed artists such as the Band and Sophie B. Hawkins, and brought in label deals such as Ruffhouse Records which resulted in albums from the Fugees, Lauryn Hill, Wyclef Jean, Kris Kross, and Cypress Hill.

The song's lyrics are written from the perspective of a woman who is observing another woman in an abusive relationship. Hawkins has explicitly stated that she is what she terms "omnisexual", which means that a lover's gender does not matter to her.[2] The song encourages people to be open-minded about this as well, with the line "Free your mind and you won't feel ashamed", which is meant to combat homophobia and queerphobia. The homoerotic nature of the song was rare at the time, as not many songs dealt with the erotic or romantic love between two women, but it was not picked up on by a large group of listeners, and it is still glossed over in many articles discussing lesbian representation in love songs.

Sophie B Hawkins was nominated as Best New Artist in 1993 and Arrested Development won! Arrested Development was formed in 1988 by rapper and producer Todd Thomas ("Speech") and turntablist Timothy Barnwell (known as Headliner). Baba Oje and frontman Speech met at the University of Wisconsin–Milwaukee when they were both students.
